figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Schematic of the experimental setup of the bubble generation system and threedimensional high-speed imaging. (b) Definition of dispersion angle α of the recorded bubble trajectories.
FIG. 4. Trajectories of bubbles rising in a chain (d = 0.5mm, ν = 1mm2/s).
TABLE I. Flat width of upward flow depends on the generation frequency f .
FIG. 17. The trajectories of bubbles in the model with increasing range of initial x−position randomness from left to right. (f = 20Hz, d = 0.5mm, ν = 1mm2/s)
FIG. 18. (left) Trajectories of bubbles in a chain near the origin and (middle and right) schematic of the two-dimensional constrained dispersion process.
FIG. 8. Relationship between bubble rising velocity and height obtained from: (a) experiment, and (b) modeling. (f = 20Hz, d = 0.5mm, Silicone oil (ν = 1mm2/s)).
FIG. 7. Trajectories of fixed diameter bubbles (d = 0.5mm) in the model. The generation frequency f increases from left to right.
FIG. 14. Definition of the geometric dispersion parameters W1 and W2 for a cross-section of the bubble chain.
FIG. 2. Schematic of the bubble chain model incorporating interactions with adjacent bubbles.
FIG. 15. Relationship between the geometric dispersion parameters W1 and W2 and the height z.

FIG. 3. Assumed upward flow profiles at different heights. The edge regions are modeled by a Gaussian distribution with a standard deviation σ = 0.5mm, while the central flat region expands with height z.
FIG. 6. Relationship between dispersion angle α and generation frequency f for different bubble diameters.
FIG. 11. Schematic of the two-way coupling between upward flow and bubbles rising in a chain.
FIG. 16. Relationship between dispersion parameter W1 and W2 and (a) generation frequency f and (b) bubble separation S.

FIG. 5. (a) Three-dimensional dispersions and (b) the corresponding bubble passage distributions in cross-sections at 20 mm intervals for bubble generation frequencies of: (i) f = 4Hz, (ii) f = 8Hz, (iii) f = 12Hz, and (iv) f = 20Hz. The centroid of the distributions in (b) is marked with a cross.

FIG. 12. Schematic of the formation mechanism of a U-shaped dispersion, with generated bubbles experiencing wake-induced lift that causes lateral migration, followed by bubbles rising vertically after exiting the wake region of a leading bubble.
FIG. 10. Schematic of the three-dimensional dispersion of bubbles rising in a chain.

FIG. 13. Formation mechanism of a V-shaped dispersion. Generated bubbles experience wakeinduced lift, causing lateral migration. After exiting the wake region of the leading bubble, bubbles experience shear-induced lift and rise obliquely.
FIG. 9. Trajectories of 0.5mm diameter bubbles in the extended model including upward flow effects. The generation frequency f increases from left to right.
